behavior_summary: ARMOR::look is the armor item's handler for the 'U8P_ARMOR::look' (examine) event. When a player/actor inspects the armor it sets item context, prevents reentrant processing for that actor, and causes the item to emit a short descriptive message beginning with the literal "armor ".

The routine constructs a temporary string (starts with "armor "), calls Item::bark to deliver that message to the inspecting process, frees the temporary string, and then inspects the bark return value before suspending/returning.
notable_control_flow: Entry via Event 0 (U8P_ARMOR::look) for the armor object.
Calls an engine 'set info' operation for the target item (uses the item handle passed via indirect 02h and the caller PID at [BP+06h]) to establish context/state.
Executes a 'process exclude' step to guard against reentrancy/duplicate handling for the calling process.
Allocates/builds the literal message string ("armor "), passes it to Item::bark, and then frees the string.
Uses an implies/pop sequence to inspect Item::bark's return value before suspending/returning control.
open_questions: What exact meaning or effect is associated with the concrete info id/context used when calling 'set info' (the constant appears as 0x0208 in the disassembly) — what game state or behavior does that flag represent?
What semantics does Item::bark's return value convey in this engine (success, interruption, or other)? The code checks it with implies/pop but the meaning is not explicit.
Are there additional localization or appended words beyond the literal prefix "armor " (e.g., shape/name concatenation) that are assembled elsewhere or by the bark call?
Does process-exclude only prevent immediate reentrancy, or does it persist longer for other systems/tasks tied to the same PID?

main_function.name: U8P_ARMOR::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player/actor examining an armor item: set item context, guard against reentrancy, and present a short descriptive message ('armor ...') to the inspecting process via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_ARMOR::look) — invoked when the avatar or actor looks at/inspects the armor item
Inferred: triggered by the armor object's U8P_ARMOR::look/observe area or U8P_ARMOR::look event handler.

function_map.name: U8P_ARMOR::look
function_map.role: Armor item 'U8P_ARMOR::look' (examine) event handler that sets item context, excludes the calling process, constructs a descriptive message and calls Item::bark.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API used to emit/display a short text message from an item/process to a target process/player.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the constructed descriptive text (starting with "armor ") to the inspecting process/player so the item 'speaks' its short description.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: ARMOR::look is the armor item's handler for the 'U8P_ARMOR::look' (examine) event. When a player/actor inspects the armor it sets item context, prevents reentrant processing for that actor, and causes the item to emit a short descriptive message beginning with the literal "armor ".
